Terms and Conditions — AI-Powered Customer Service

1. AGREEMENT
This proposal, upon acceptance by Punjab IT Board, constitutes a binding agreement between the parties. The engagement shall commence upon receipt of the signed Statement of Work and initial payment.

2. PAYMENT TERMS
All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Punjab IT Board shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for Punjab IT Board under this engagement shall become the property of Punjab IT Board upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, Punjab IT Board shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This proposal complies with PPRA Rules 2004 of Pakistan. Transparent pricing, competitive documentation, and procurement audit trails are provided as required by the Public Procurement Regulatory Authority.

Compliance Framework: PPRA

10. PPRA-SPECIFIC PROVISIONS
  - All pricing is transparent and documented for audit purposes
  - Competitive bidding documentation is maintained
  - Procurement processes comply with PPRA Rules 2004
  - Full audit trail is available for regulatory review
  - Tax compliance includes 17% GST as applicable

Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, SkyBridge Analytics shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This engagement adheres to SOC 2 Type II security standards and GDPR data protection requirements. All data processing occurs within certified environments with full encryption at rest and in transit.

Compliance Framework: SOC 2, GDPR

10. SOC 2 & GDPR PROVISIONS
  - Service delivery adheres to SOC 2 Type II trust service criteria
  - Annual SOC 2 audit reports are available upon request
  - GDPR compliance is maintained for any EU data subject information
  - Data encryption standards: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
  - Access controls follow principle of least privilege

Terms and Conditions — Infrastructure Modernization

1. AGREEMENT
This proposal, upon acceptance by Karachi Chamber of Commerce, constitutes a binding agreement between the parties. The engagement shall commence upon receipt of the signed Statement of Work and initial payment.

2. PAYMENT TERMS
All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Karachi Chamber of Commerce shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for Karachi Chamber of Commerce under this engagement shall become the property of Karachi Chamber of Commerce upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, Karachi Chamber of Commerce shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This proposal complies with PPRA Rules 2004 of Pakistan. Transparent pricing, competitive documentation, and procurement audit trails are provided as required by the Public Procurement Regulatory Authority.

Terms and Conditions — Infrastructure Modernization

1. AGREEMENT
This proposal, upon acceptance by Pacific Logistics Corp, constitutes a binding agreement between the parties. The engagement shall commence upon receipt of the signed Statement of Work and initial payment.

2. PAYMENT TERMS
All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Pacific Logistics Corp shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for Pacific Logistics Corp under this engagement shall become the property of Pacific Logistics Corp upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, Pacific Logistics Corp shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This engagement adheres to SOC 2 Type II security standards and GDPR data protection requirements. All data processing occurs within certified environments with full encryption at rest and in transit.

Compliance Framework: SOC 2, GDPR

10. SOC 2 & GDPR PROVISIONS
  - Service delivery adheres to SOC 2 Type II trust service criteria
  - Annual SOC 2 audit reports are available upon request
  - GDPR compliance is maintained for any EU data subject information
  - Data encryption standards: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
  - Access controls follow principle of least privilege

All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Pakistan Federal Ministry of IT shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for Pakistan Federal Ministry of IT under this engagement shall become the property of Pakistan Federal Ministry of IT upon full payment.

Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, Pakistan Federal Ministry of IT shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.

This proposal complies with PPRA Rules 2004 of Pakistan. Transparent pricing, competitive documentation, and procurement audit trails are provided as required by the Public Procurement Regulatory Authority.

Terms and Conditions — Data Analytics Suite

1. AGREEMENT
This proposal, upon acceptance by Karachi Chamber of Commerce, constitutes a binding agreement between the parties. The engagement shall commence upon receipt of the signed Statement of Work and initial payment.

2. PAYMENT TERMS
All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Karachi Chamber of Commerce shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for Karachi Chamber of Commerce under this engagement shall become the property of Karachi Chamber of Commerce upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, Karachi Chamber of Commerce shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This proposal complies with PPRA Rules 2004 of Pakistan. Transparent pricing, competitive documentation, and procurement audit trails are provided as required by the Public Procurement Regulatory Authority.

Terms and Conditions — Document Management System

1. AGREEMENT
This proposal, upon acceptance by Vertex Financial Group, constitutes a binding agreement between the parties. The engagement shall commence upon receipt of the signed Statement of Work and initial payment.

2. PAYMENT TERMS
All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Vertex Financial Group shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for Vertex Financial Group under this engagement shall become the property of Vertex Financial Group upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, Vertex Financial Group shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
Processing of personal data complies with GDPR (EU) 2016/679. A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is included. Data subjects retain all rights including erasure, portability, and access.

Compliance Framework: GDPR

10. GDPR-SPECIFIC PROVISIONS
  - A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is attached as Appendix A
  - Data subjects retain rights to access, rectification, and erasure
  - Data processing is limited to the minimum necessary for service delivery
  - Cross-border data transfers comply with Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs)

Terms and Conditions — ERP Integration Project

1. AGREEMENT
This proposal, upon acceptance by Meridian Consulting Group, constitutes a binding agreement between the parties. The engagement shall commence upon receipt of the signed Statement of Work and initial payment.

2. PAYMENT TERMS
All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Meridian Consulting Group shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for Meridian Consulting Group under this engagement shall become the property of Meridian Consulting Group upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, Meridian Consulting Group shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This engagement adheres to SOC 2 Type II security standards and GDPR data protection requirements. All data processing occurs within certified environments with full encryption at rest and in transit.

Compliance Framework: SOC 2, GDPR

10. SOC 2 & GDPR PROVISIONS
  - Service delivery adheres to SOC 2 Type II trust service criteria
  - Annual SOC 2 audit reports are available upon request
  - GDPR compliance is maintained for any EU data subject information
  - Data encryption standards: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
  - Access controls follow principle of least privilege

Terms and Conditions — SaaS Platform Migration

1. AGREEMENT
This proposal, upon acceptance by Acme Corporation, constitutes a binding agreement between the parties. The engagement shall commence upon receipt of the signed Statement of Work and initial payment.

2. PAYMENT TERMS
All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Acme Corporation shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for Acme Corporation under this engagement shall become the property of Acme Corporation upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, Acme Corporation shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This engagement adheres to SOC 2 Type II security standards and GDPR data protection requirements. All data processing occurs within certified environments with full encryption at rest and in transit.

All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for SkyBridge Analytics under this engagement shall become the property of SkyBridge Analytics upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, SkyBridge Analytics shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This engagement adheres to SOC 2 Type II security standards and GDPR data protection requirements. All data processing occurs within certified environments with full encryption at rest and in transit.

Compliance Framework: SOC 2, GDPR

10. SOC 2 & GDPR PROVISIONS
  - Service delivery adheres to SOC 2 Type II trust service criteria
  - Annual SOC 2 audit reports are available upon request
  - GDPR compliance is maintained for any EU data subject information
  - Data encryption standards: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
  - Access controls follow principle of least privilege

All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Pakistan Federal Ministry of IT shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for Pakistan Federal Ministry of IT under this engagement shall become the property of Pakistan Federal Ministry of IT upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, Pakistan Federal Ministry of IT shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This proposal complies with PPRA Rules 2004 of Pakistan. Transparent pricing, competitive documentation, and procurement audit trails are provided as required by the Public Procurement Regulatory Authority.

Terms and Conditions — ERP Integration Project

1. AGREEMENT
This proposal, upon acceptance by Punjab IT Board, constitutes a binding agreement between the parties. The engagement shall commence upon receipt of the signed Statement of Work and initial payment.

2. PAYMENT TERMS
All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Punjab IT Board shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for Punjab IT Board under this engagement shall become the property of Punjab IT Board upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, Punjab IT Board shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This proposal complies with PPRA Rules 2004 of Pakistan. Transparent pricing, competitive documentation, and procurement audit trails are provided as required by the Public Procurement Regulatory Authority.

Compliance Framework: PPRA

10. PPRA-SPECIFIC PROVISIONS
  - All pricing is transparent and documented for audit purposes
  - Competitive bidding documentation is maintained
  - Procurement processes comply with PPRA Rules 2004
  - Full audit trail is available for regulatory review
  - Tax compliance includes 17% GST as applicable

Terms and Conditions — Marketing Automation Platform

1. AGREEMENT
This proposal, upon acceptance by RedLine Security, constitutes a binding agreement between the parties. The engagement shall commence upon receipt of the signed Statement of Work and initial payment.

2. PAYMENT TERMS
All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. RedLine Security shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for RedLine Security under this engagement shall become the property of RedLine Security upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, RedLine Security shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This engagement adheres to SOC 2 Type II security standards and GDPR data protection requirements. All data processing occurs within certified environments with full encryption at rest and in transit.

  - Service delivery adheres to SOC 2 Type II trust service criteria

1. AGREEMENT

Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.

Terms and Conditions — Marketing Automation Platform

1. AGREEMENT
This proposal, upon acceptance by Oasis Retail Group, constitutes a binding agreement between the parties. The engagement shall commence upon receipt of the signed Statement of Work and initial payment.

2. PAYMENT TERMS
All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date (Net 30). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Oasis Retail Group shall notify the provider of any disputed charges within ten (10) business days.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All pre-existing intellectual property remains with its respective owner. Custom deliverables created specifically for Oasis Retail Group under this engagement shall become the property of Oasis Retail Group upon full payment.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information exchanged during this engagement. Confidential information shall not be disclosed to third parties without prior written consent. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

5. WARRANTY
All deliverables are warranted for a period of twelve (12) months from acceptance. During the warranty period, defects in functionality will be remedied at no additional charge.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Total aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

7. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, Oasis Retail Group shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date.

8. FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, or infrastructure failures.
9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
Processing of personal data complies with GDPR (EU) 2016/679. A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is included. Data subjects retain all rights including erasure, portability, and access.

Compliance Framework: GDPR

10. GDPR-SPECIFIC PROVISIONS
  - A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is attached as Appendix A
  - Data subjects retain rights to access, rectification, and erasure
  - Data processing is limited to the minimum necessary for service delivery
  - Cross-border data transfers comply with Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs)
  - Data breach notification will occur within 72 hours of discovery
